chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.D-2108 of 2025. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objections. For hearing of main case. 24.02.2026. Mr. Muntazir Mehdi Laghari, Advocate for Petitioners. Mr. Zulfiqar Ali Korai, Advocate for Respondent No.3 to 5 alongwith Zubair Ahmed Pathan (CFO_WASA). Mr. Naeem Uddin Sahito, Advocate for Respondent No.6 (HDA). Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. ***************************************** The Petitioners have approached this Court seeking release of their service-related dues, including monthly pension, gratuity / commutation, LPR, GP Fund, leave encashment and arrears of salary. 2. Learned counsel for Respondent No.3 to 5 is present alongwith the Chief Finance Officer, WASA, and has filed a detailed statement accompanied by a comprehensive payment schedule pertaining to both Petitioners. A copy of the same has been supplied to learned counsel for the petitioners, who expresses satisfaction but submits that the monthly pension and salary of the petitioners have not yet commenced. Upon this submission, learned counsel for Respondent No.3 to 5, in the presence of the Chief Finance Officer, undertakes that the monthly pension of both petitioners shall commence from March, 2026. 3. The payment schedule filed by the Chief Finance Officer reflects that in respect of Petitioner No.1 (Muhammad Rafiq Khan), the outstanding monthly pension from October, 2025 to Jan, 2026 at the rate of Rs.131,993/- for four months total Rs.527,972/- shall be paid in two monthly installments of Rs.263,986/- each, commencing from March, 2026 to April, 2026.

Upon this submission, learned counsel for Respondent No.3 to 5, in the presence of the Chief Finance Officer, undertakes that the monthly pension of both petitioners shall commence from March, 2026. 3. The payment schedule filed by the Chief Finance Officer reflects that in respect of Petitioner No.1 (Muhammad Rafiq Khan), the outstanding monthly pension from October, 2025 to Jan, 2026 at the rate of Rs.131,993/- for four months total Rs.527,972/- shall be paid in two monthly installments of Rs.263,986/- each, commencing from March, 2026 to April, 2026. His Page 2 of 2 Commutation (share of HW&SC) amount i.e. Rs.3,609,302/- is to be paid in twelve equal monthly installments of Rs.300,775/- each beginning from May, 2026 to April, 2027. His LPR amount Rs.1,279,560/- shall be paid in four equal monthly installments commencing from May, 2027 to August, 2027. Commutation (share of P&DC, HDA) Rs.192,608/- and Commutation (Share of Main Sectt. HDA) Rs.200,982/- shall be paid by HDA directly. While his G.P Fund has still not finalized. 4. Further the payment schedule filed by the Chief Finance Officer reflects that in respect of Petitioner No.2 (Ghulam Qambar), the outstanding monthly pension from October, 2025 to Jan, 2026 at the rate of Rs.89,571/- for four months total Rs.358,284/- shall be paid in two equal monthly installments of Rs.179,142/- each, commencing from March, 2026 to April, 2026. His Commutation share WASA/HW&SC amount i.e. Rs.2,716,332/- is to be paid in eight equal monthly installments of Rs.339,542/- each, beginning from May, 2026 to December, 2026. His G.P Fund amount Rs.1,235,084/- shall be paid in four equal monthly installments of Rs.308,771/- commencing from January, 2027 to April, 2027. While his Commutation share of Housing project, HDA Rs.191,967/-, Commutation share of Main Sectt:, HDA Rs.3,839/- and LPR shall be paid by HDA directly. 5. In view of the detailed schedule submitted and the undertaking that monthly pension shall commence from March, 2026, the instant petition alongwith pending application(s), stands disposed of with directions to Respondent No.3 to 5 to strictly comply with the payment schedule in its entirety, in letter and spirit.

While his Commutation share of Housing project, HDA Rs.191,967/-, Commutation share of Main Sectt:, HDA Rs.3,839/- and LPR shall be paid by HDA directly. 5. In view of the detailed schedule submitted and the undertaking that monthly pension shall commence from March, 2026, the instant petition alongwith pending application(s), stands disposed of with directions to Respondent No.3 to 5 to strictly comply with the payment schedule in its entirety, in letter and spirit. Any deviation, delay or non-compliance shall expose the responsible officials to contempt proceedings before this Court. JUDGE JUDGE Ali.
